summary: Iteration 2 e2e testing of FATELYN new features (JWT auth, swiss-ephemeris astrology, admin panel, protected routes). Backend: 18/18 pytest passing (register+cookies, admin login, /me, logout, refresh, 401 on wrong password, chart with 9 planets incl Ketu and 12 houses, panchang with real hh:mm times, dasha with ISO date ranges, gochar, admin dashboard w/ 7-day series, users list/patch role/delete, activity, content GET/PUT hero_headline, prompts GET/PUT quest_system, 403 gate for non-admin, 401 for anonymous). Frontend: /login (all 5 data-testids present, admin login → /admin), /signup (all testids, submit → /dashboard), /admin (sidebar loads, all 7 tabs clickable — see minor selector mismatch, non-admin redirected to /dashboard), /dashboard (birth-save-btn triggers real /api/astro/chart+dasha, renders Ascendant=Leo, Moon Purva Phalguni, all 9 planets w/ signs/nakshatra/houses/degrees, Panchang with sunrise 05:28/sunset 19:23/moonrise 22:47/moonset 10:04), Landing (FATELYN branding, nav-login-link + nav-signup-btn present when logged out), /prototype and /showcase regression pass.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout is per-pod. Identifier is `client_ip:email` where client_ip is `request.client.host` as seen by the backend replica. In this K8s deployment requests are load-balanced across pods (observed 10.219.2.4 and 10.219.2.7 in login_attempts collection) so the per-pod counter often never reaches 5. Lockout works only when consecutive attempts hit the same pod. Fix: use MongoDB atomic $inc + centralized store (already the case) AND compose identifier without client IP (or use X-Forwarded-For / just email), so the counter is truly global.

ui_bugs.issue: Spec expects data-testid `admin-tab-subscriptions` but code uses key `subs` → actual testid is `admin-tab-subs`. Tab is functionally present and clickable. Either rename the key to `subscriptions` or update spec.

action_items: Rename AdminPanel tab key `subs` → `subscriptions` for consistent selector `admin-tab-subscriptions`.

Harden brute-force lockout so the counter is global across pods: drop `client.host` from the identifier (use email only) or use X-Forwarded-For from ingress. Currently pod-sharded so 5-fails-in-15-min policy is weaker than intended.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py brute-force identifier uses `request.client.host` — behind K8s ingress this is the pod-local socket peer and is not a stable client identity; combined with multi-pod deployment the lockout is bypassable. Recommend switching to `X-Forwarded-For` (first hop) or removing IP entirely.
auth.py `_check_lockout` reads `locked_until` but does not re-issue a fresh lock or reset counter once the window expires; acceptable but consider TTL index on login_attempts.at.
server.py Quest AI: LLM budget errors are surfaced as 502 with raw exception string in detail (line ~96) — acceptable for now, sanitize before production.
AdminPanel tab key inconsistency (`subs` vs Subscriptions) — small nit, worth aligning for automated tests and analytics.

astro.py: `_local_to_utc` builds a datetime with tzinfo=utc then subtracts hours — this works but is semantically confusing; a `timezone(timedelta(hours=tz_offset))` on the local datetime would be clearer.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@fatelyn.app / fatelyn@admin2026 (seeded on startup, verified working). Test users created dynamically per-run with prefix `test_<hex>@fatelyn.app` / `test123456`.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral test users only (auto-created per test via /api/auth/register). Admin user auto-seeded by backend startup hook — verified idempotent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cookies are httpOnly + secure + samesite=none — use requests.Session with withCredentials-equivalent for backend tests. Brute-force test is flaky due to pod-sharded counter (xfail'd). AdminPanel subscriptions tab has key `subs` (testid `admin-tab-subs`, NOT `admin-tab-subscriptions`). UserDashboard birth-save-btn triggers real swiss-ephemeris compute — allow ~3-5s after clicking before asserting DOM. Signup default password `test123456` (>=6 chars) satisfies backend validator.
rca of the issue: Brute-force per-pod: /api/auth/login records failed attempts keyed by `f'{request.client.host}:{email}'`. In the preview K8s deployment there are multiple backend pods behind ingress; each request may land on a different pod and the recorded `client.host` differs (verified in login_attempts collection: entries for both 10.219.2.4 and 10.219.2.7 for same email with counts 2 and 4 respectively). Because the count is per-identifier and identifier includes the pod-observed IP, no single identifier ever reaches the 5-attempt threshold consistently. Fix: use only `email` (or a stable forwarded-for header) as the lockout key.
